title: Are there any other options for outputting or exporting a table

posts.author: iankerry (Ian Kerry)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

I am new to Monday.com and am loving it, though i have lots to learn.

I wondered if there was a better way of sharing my board - and by this I mean I have to share it with a funder, and upload it to their portal - so I can’t just given them access to it.
Here is a screenshot of part of a huge board:

image1811×334 49 KB

I have tried to export to excel, and whilst it works, it looks awful and i then have to spend an hour or more making it vaguely readable.

Is there another way? is there an add on I can use?

Any thoughts appreciated.

Ian

posts.author: CharlotteK (Charlotte Kirkham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Ian!

We have just the thing!

Tada! Here it is: How to share a board view publicly

You can link or embed a board so they can see the specific info from the board that you want to share without being able to access the board or account.

Let me know if this works for what you need!

posts.author: iankerry (Ian Kerry)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Charlotte, many thanks for your reply.

This isn’t quite what i need though. The funder has a portal where i have to upload lots of documents including my monday,com board. So it would have to be an export of some kind, but one that keeps all the formatting. but thanks for your time,
ian

posts.author: MASO (Markus @ Gorilla Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @iankerry

you might want to have a look at our app Board to Website Widget. It does not provide the perfect workflow for your use case and subitems are not supported at this point, but it could work out like this:

  1. Configure your widget settings once with a table layout
  2. Publish the widget once in a while with updated items
  3. Open the share link in your browser and Right Click → Save As → Complete Web Page (Chrome browser)
  4. Compress the files (zip) and upload it to your funders portal

The only downside to this would be that icons won’t be displayed properly due to domain restrictions. But if that doesn’t matter, you might want to give it a try. Take a look at our demos, to get a better feeling on how you can turn boards into website widgets.

I hope this can be a workaround for you. Let me know what you think.

– Markus

P.S. If a raw Excel file is a viable option, you may take a look at Exported Excel Cleaner, which is free for small teams

posts.author: iankerry (Ian Kerry)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi Marcus,

many thanks for replying and offering some options.

Whilst the table layout could work, especially if it is developed so that subitems can be done, the cost may be an issue for us,

however the excel one i am keen to try! So tanks very much for that.

best wishes

ian

Ian

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Ian,
Alternatively, you can auto-import your board to the Smart Spreadsheet app and get every group and subitems on separate tabs, which makes it easier to analyze and read board data in a spreadsheet rather than native export.

posts.author: iankerry (Ian Kerry)
posts.cookedHtml: Many thanks Iryna, i have subscribed for the trial. i haven’t managed to auto import yet though. I think though that unless it auto-syncs with the board, it isnt going to work? The monday board is the way staff engage with it, and all i need to do is find a good looking way of sharing the board with our stakeholders, and in a way it can be uploaded to their funding website. But thanks!

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @iankerry ,
Thank your for your interest. This is the document that explains how to auto-import your board into a spreadsheet, which then will be synced with a board. Then you can export the board and share it with your stakeholders. Please, freely reach out to me if you need a free demo or have other questions.

posts.author: MichaelTomar (Michael Tomar)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Ian,

Welcome to Monday! It’s great to hear that you’re enjoying the platform.

For a more automated and professional approach, you can try tools like Skyvia. It is a no-code platform that allows seamless integration with Monday and other systems. It can export your Monday data into structured and readable formats and sync the data with other platforms like Excel, Google Sheets, or databases in near real-time.

You can read more about this integration here.

Hope it helps

posts.author: iankerry (Ian Kerry)
posts.cookedHtml:
Many thanks Michael, I’l give that a look.

Best wishes

Ian
